Jwt._comment: Development uses the local RS256 key provider (KeyVaultUri is empty). Set DevKeyPath to a PEM file to use a stable key across restarts; otherwise an ephemeral RSA-2048 key is generated at startup. Generate one with: openssl genrsa -out jwt-dev-signing.pem 2048 (the *.pem path is git-ignored).

Authentication._comment: UseStub keeps the deterministic StubSsoService in development, where no real Google/Microsoft OAuth credentials exist. Set to false once real client id/secret are placed in the Authentication:Google / Authentication:Microsoft sections to use the real SsoService.

FileScanning._comment: Off in development — no ClamAV daemon runs locally, so the NoOpFileScanService is used. To exercise real scanning locally, run the clamav docker-compose service and set Enabled=true.

FieldEncryption._comment: DEV-ONLY fixed key. Field encryption needs a key that is STABLE across restarts — an ephemeral key would make every previously-encrypted row permanently unreadable — so this is a fixed Base64 256-bit AES key, not generated at startup. This well-known value is acceptable for development only; production leaves DevKey empty and sets KeyVaultUri so the key is read from Azure Key Vault.
